sections.html: These are your colleagues at NEXUS Security. You’ll hear from them throughout the semester — in lab callouts, mentor quotes, and guidance sections. They’re fictional, but the lessons they teach are real.

sections.label: Marcus Chen — Founder & Principal Consultant
sections.tagline: Strategic security · Executive communication · Risk management
paragraphs: Twenty years in security, former NSA, founded NEXUS after corporate burnout. Marcus sets the strategic direction for engagements and handles executive-level client relationships. Calm, philosophical, and good at seeing the big picture — you won’t hear from him often, but when you do, listen.
sections.quote: Security isn’t about being unhackable. It’s about making the cost of attacking you higher than the value of what you’re protecting.

sections.label: Maya Rodriguez — Senior Penetration Tester
sections.tagline: Red team operations · Offensive methodology · OSINT
paragraphs: Red team specialist with a talent for finding what others miss. Maya leads offensive engagements and mentors junior analysts on methodology. She’s methodical, technical, and loves the hunt — her training sessions are challenging but fair, and she pushes you to dig deeper.
sections.quote: Tools are just tools. A script kiddie runs nmap and calls it a day. A professional understands what every flag does and why.

sections.label: Carmen Vega — Junior Security Analyst
sections.tagline: Peer perspective · OSINT research · Client prep
paragraphs: Your peer at NEXUS — she started six months before you. Carmen handles OSINT research and client prep. She’s been where you are, made the same mistakes, and learned from them. She’ll share research openly and warn you about the common pitfalls before you trip over them.
sections.quote: I miscalculated a subnet mask once and filtered out half my capture. Do the math right the first time.

sections.label: Derek Mitchell — Senior Security Engineer (Blue Team Lead)
paragraphs: Former SOC analyst and incident responder, now focused on defensive architecture. Derek oversees SOC workflows, incident response procedures, and defensive tooling. Methodical, patient, teaches by asking questions, dry sense of humor — he’s the one who builds the playbooks that stop the attacks Maya finds.
sections.quote: Attackers have to be right once. Defenders have to be right every time. That’s why we layer defenses.

sections.label: Maria Delgado, PhD — Security Compliance Specialist
paragraphs: Bridges the gap between technical findings and regulatory requirements. Maria translates NEXUS engagements into HIPAA, PCI-DSS, and CMMC compliance language. Academic precision meets practical experience — she’s the one who makes sure your report recommendations actually map to what the client’s auditors will check.
sections.quote: Cryptography isn’t magic — it’s math. And math done wrong is worse than no math at all because it gives false confidence.

sections.label: Note:
sections.html: You’ll also interact with client stakeholders throughout the semester. Check the Our Clients page to learn about the businesses you’ll be working with.
Your team has your back. Now let’s get to work.
